other_observers: Bill and Jim Schmoker, Brandon Percival; Joey Kellner, and other Denver observers on Jan. 1
description: This large loon was seen next to two smaller and smaller-billed Common Loons. The key features are the ivory-colored bill, upturned some of the time with a dark basal half to the culmen, the whitish face with a dark smudge on one of the auriculars, and whitish circles around the beady eyes. The sides of the neck were buffy, the chin, throat, and upper breast white.Behaviors: Sent much of its time underwater. No signs of it catching any. Traveled long distances during the 40 minutes I watched it.Call: none heardPlumage: 1st winter
